task_completion.description: Survey is created with the correct title and Assessment Mode is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'Copenhagen Burnout Inventory - Workplace Wellness 2024' in the LimeSurvey interface. In the survey settings (often under Data management or General settings), verify that 'Assessment mode' is toggled on or set to 'Yes'.

task_completion.description: A question group named 'Personal Burnout Scale' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a group titled 'Personal Burnout Scale' in the survey structure menu on the left sidebar or in the group list.

task_completion.description: A mandatory Array-type question is created with the correct code and text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a question with code 'PB', type 'Array', marked as mandatory (often indicated by a red asterisk or a mandatory toggle set to 'On'). The question text should read: 'Over the past two weeks, please rate how often you have experienced each of the following:'.

task_completion.description: Six specific sub-questions (PB01-PB06) are added to the array question.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the sub-questions configuration for question PB. There should be exactly 6 rows with codes PB01 through PB06, containing the text about feeling tired, physically exhausted, emotionally exhausted, etc.

task_completion.description: Five answer options are added with specific assessment values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the answer options configuration for question PB. There should be 5 options (AO01-AO05) with text ranging from 'Never/almost never' to 'Always'. Crucially, check the 'Assessment value' column for these options: they must be set to 0, 25, 50, 75, and 100 respectively.

task_completion.description: Three assessment rules are configured with correct score ranges and feedback.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Assessments' or 'Assessment rules' page for the survey. Verify there are 3 distinct rules. Check the Minimum and Maximum score columns: Rule 1 (0 to 200), Rule 2 (201 to 400), and Rule 3 (401 to 600). The corresponding feedback text should match the Low, Moderate, and High burnout descriptions.
Look for a status indicator showing the survey is 'Active' or 'Running', or the presence of a 'Stop this survey' button instead of an 'Activate this survey' button in the top toolbar.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the LimeSurvey web GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LimeSurvey admin interface (menus, toolbars, form fields) in a web browser, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to mock the survey structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The survey configuration must be done inside the LimeSurvey application, not by writing a .lss or .txt file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Assessment rules were entered into the actual LimeSurvey assessment module.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rules should be visible in the standard LimeSurvey assessment table format, not just pasted as plain text into a generic description field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The survey must be titled 'Copenhagen Burnout Inventory - Workplace Wellness 2024'. It requires exactly 6 sub-questions (PB01-PB06) matching the Copenhagen Burnout Inventory Personal Burnout Scale. The answer options must use a 5-point scale with assessment values explicitly set to: 0, 25, 50, 75, 100. Exactly 3 assessment rules must be configured with raw score ranges: 0-200 (Low Burnout), 201-400 (Moderate Burnout), and 401-600 (High Burnout).
